_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development/eval. Colonoscopy result for positive-FIT diagnostic workup in a high-acuity cardiac patient (HFrEF EF 25%, ICD, new AFib on apixaban).

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Positive fecal immunochemical test (FIT) — diagnostic colonoscopy
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol) with anesthesiology present; selected over endoscopist-directed moderate sedation given HFrEF EF 25% and ICD. ICD managed per EP plan (magnet on standby, no reprogramming required for lower endoscopy). Continuous hemodynamic and telemetry monitoring.
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 7/9 (right 2, transverse 2, left 3) — adequate; low-volume split-dose PEG used with attention to electrolyte and volume status given HFrEF and reduced-dose spironolactone
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice, ileocecal valve, and terminal ileum intubation)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with polypectomy. INDICATION: positive FIT (2026-06-28) in a 58-year-old woman with ischemic cardiomyopathy, HFrEF (EF 25%), single-chamber ICD, and new-onset atrial fibrillation. ANTICOAGULATION: apixaban 5 mg BID held for 3 days pre-procedure per plan coordinated with cardiology/EP (last dose morning of 2026-08-03); low-dose aspirin 81 mg continued through the procedure. SEDATION: monitored anesthesia care with anesthesiology present given cardiac risk; ICD managed per EP plan. PREP: adequate (BBPS 7). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and appeared normal. A 9 mm pedunculated polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by snare polypectomy; a prophylactic hemoclip was placed on the stalk given interrupted anticoagulation. A 6 mm sessile polyp in the sigmoid colon was removed by cold snare. Two diminutive (2-3 mm) polyps in the descending colon were removed by cold biopsy forceps. Non-bleeding sigmoid diverticulosis was noted without stigmata of recent hemorrhage. Internal hemorrhoids, small, non-bleeding. No mass, no ulceration, and no active bleeding identified. All specimens retrieved and sent to pathology. IMPRESSION: Multiple colon polyps removed (ascending 9 mm pedunculated, sigmoid 6 mm, two descending diminutive); diverticulosis; internal hemorrhoids. Positive FIT most likely attributable to the polyps; no other bleeding source identified. Pathology pending. Given interrupted therapeutic anticoagulation and clip placement, delayed post-polypectomy bleeding risk counseled.

findings.action_taken: snare polypectomy, retrieved; prophylactic hemoclip placed on stalk

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after extended monitored recovery given cardiac history; hemodynamically stable, at cardiac baseline, no arrhythmia during recovery. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed, including delayed-bleeding warning signs given interrupted anticoagulation. Instructed NOT to restart apixaban until explicitly cleared by GI/cardiology.

followup.recommendation: Review pathology on all polypectomy specimens (ascending 9 mm, sigmoid 6 mm, descending x2); call patient with results and set surveillance interval per histology/size/number

Resume apixaban 5 mg BID after post-polypectomy bleeding risk window, coordinated with cardiology/EP — clip placed on ascending stalk; balance stroke prophylaxis (new AFib, elevated CHA2DS2-VASc, EF 25%) against delayed bleeding. Confirm exact restart day and communicate to patient in writing
Continue low-dose aspirin 81 mg (was not held); confirm patient understands aspirin and apixaban are separate
Recheck BMP (potassium, creatinine) post-prep/post-procedure given prior K 5.4, reduced-dose spironolactone 12.5 mg, and HFrEF; ensure euvolemia
Communicate procedure outcome and device course to cardiology/EP; ICD tolerated procedure without event

planted_followup_items.description: Histology pending on all four polypectomy specimens (ascending 9 mm pedunculated, sigmoid 6 mm, two descending diminutive). Result determines whether any advanced histology (villous features, high-grade dysplasia) is present and drives the surveillance interval; must be communicated to the patient.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with pathology result and document surveillance interval per histology, size, and polyp count

planted_followup_items.description: Therapeutic apixaban 5 mg BID was held 3 days pre-procedure and remains held post-polypectomy. Patient has new-onset atrial fibrillation with elevated CHA2DS2-VASc and EF 25% (high stroke risk) but underwent multiple polypectomies including a pedunculated stalk with clip during interrupted anticoagulation (delayed-bleeding risk). Restart timing must be set and communicated in writing; if dropped, patient is left off stroke prophylaxis indefinitely or restarts unsafely.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: GI and cardiology/EP jointly set and document exact apixaban restart date; give patient written instructions; confirm resumption occurred

planted_followup_items.description: Multiple polyps removed; surveillance colonoscopy interval must be set once pathology returns (contingent on F1). Positive-FIT diagnostic exam with adenomatous polyps typically warrants a shortened interval.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: assign and document next colonoscopy interval after pathology; enter into recall/reminder system

planted_followup_items.description: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding risk elevated due to interrupted therapeutic anticoagulation, pedunculated ascending polyp with stalk resection (clip placed), and continued aspirin. Bleeding can present up to ~2 weeks post-procedure.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: patient counseled on hematochezia/melena/lightheadedness; ensure return precautions and a reachable contact; reassess before apixaban restart

planted_followup_items.description: Reduced-dose spironolactone (12.5 mg) with prior hyperkalemia (K 5.4), creatinine 1.3, and HFrEF; bowel prep and periprocedural fasting can shift electrolytes/volume, warranting post-procedure electrolyte monitoring.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: recheck BMP post-procedure; coordinate with primary care/cardiology on electrolyte and volume management

planted_followup_items.description: Close-the-loop with cardiology/EP: ICD tolerated the procedure without arrhythmic event; anticoagulation was held periprocedurally. Cardiology/EP must be informed to co-manage apixaban restart and confirm device status.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: send procedure summary to cardiology/EP; confirm receipt and shared plan for apixaban resumption

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. Fully synthetic.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I'm really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and colonic insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I take two blood thinners — the little aspirin and the twice-a-day apixaban. I kept taking the aspirin. When exactly do I start the apixaban again? I don't want to miss a dose and have a stroke.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient seeking apixaban restart timing after held therapeutic anticoagulation; correctly distinguishing continued aspirin from held apixaban
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: aspirin was intentionally continued; apixaban restart date must be set jointly by GI and cardiology/EP given clip placement and high stroke risk. Provide the written restart date and confirm she has it; do not have her restart on her own until instructed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable/coordination item that maps to the critical med_resume follow-up (F2); no clinical red flag but must not be dropped.

contacts.verbatim: I've passed a bunch of dark red blood into the toilet three times this evening and there are clots. I feel dizzy and clammy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage with hemodynamic symptoms; higher risk given resected pedunculated stalk, continued aspirin, and pending apixaban restart timing
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; do NOT restart apixaban; alert GI on-call and anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis; notify cardiology given anticoagulation/EF context.
contacts.rationale: Hematochezia with clots plus orthostatic lightheadedness is delayed post-polypectomy bleeding with a hemodynamic signal — a true complication requiring emergent action; links to complication_watch (F4).

contacts.verbatim: Nobody has called me yet about the results of the polyps you took out. Should I have heard by now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient prompting on outstanding pathology communication
contacts.expected_action: Confirm pathology status (expected back 2026-08-11); ensure a GI team member calls with the result and the surveillance interval, and document the loop is closed.
contacts.rationale: Benign informational request that maps to the pending_pathology item (F1); ensures the result is not lost to follow-up.
